title: acampe pachyglossa subsp. reschiana
intro: Acampe pachyglossa subsp. reschiana, also known as the Reschiana Acampe, is a rare species of orchid native to the Himalayas. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be beneficial in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Acampe pachyglossa subsp. reschiana include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in traditional Ayurvedic medicine, often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
